2011-08-01.title: Boat Runs Into Barge in Moscow, Killing 9
2011-08-01.abstract: A passenger boat collided with a barge in a bend in the Moscow River not far from the Kremlin on Sunday, three weeks after a major cruise boat disaster on the Volga River killed 122 people.

2011-08-01.abstract: José Antonio Acosta Hernández, accused of masterminding the killings of three people connected to an American consulate in northern Mexico, has been captured, Mexico’s federal police said.

2011-08-01.abstract: The attorney general of Afghanistan has taken the first steps to prosecute those involved in a scheme to defraud Kabul Bank of hundreds of millions of dollars.

2011-08-01.abstract: The populist, grass-roots challenge to the government over the cost of living could open new political terrain in Israel.

2011-08-01.title: Japanese Find Radioactivity on Their Own
2011-08-01.abstract: As the government fumbles its reaction to the widespread contamination, residents are using their own dosimeters.

2011-08-03.abstract: In the heat of the summer, “plagues” of locusts or scorpions or snakes, real or merely rumored, tend to get blown up into apocalyptic panics in Georgia.

2011-08-03.abstract: Beleaguered Iraqis are struggling with the convergence of Ramadan and a heat wave.

2011-08-05.abstract: At least 16 babies were seized by family planning officials from 1999 to 2006 in Hunan Province; parents say the babies were used as a source of revenue.

2011-08-27.abstract: Rebels investigated the Abu Salim Prison, and dozens of bodies were piled up in an abandoned hospital in Tripoli.

2011-08-27.title: China: North Korean Leader Repeats Offer for Nuclear Talks
2011-08-27.abstract: Kim Jong-il reiterated Friday that he would return to the so-called six-party talks on his country’s nuclear program with no preconditions, the Chinese state news agency reported.

2011-08-27.title: Libyan War Goes a Long Way to Improve the Pentagon’s View of France as an Ally
2011-08-27.abstract: France played a major role in this war, winning grudging respect from a Pentagon long critical of European allies.

2011-08-28.title: Battle for Libya | August 27, 2011
2011-08-28.abstract: Libyans examined the Bab al-Aziziya complex, which housed Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi and his family in Tripoli.

2011-08-28.title: No Favorite Emerges in Race for Japanese Premier
2011-08-28.abstract: The vote is Monday, but most of the candidates are relative unknowns and seem to be short on concrete ideas.

2011-08-28.title: Vettel Returns to Victory, at Belgian Grand Prix
2011-08-28.abstract: Sebastian Vettel won again at Spa-Francorchamps after a drought of three races.

2011-08-28.title: Turkish Leader Says He Has Lost Confidence in Assad
2011-08-28.abstract: President Abdullah Gul of Turkey stopped just short of calling for Syria’s president, Bashar al-Assad, to step down.

2011-08-28.title: Lockerbie Planner Reported Near Death
2011-08-28.abstract: The mastermind of the 1988 bombing of Pan Am 103, which was blown up over Lockerbie, Scotland, in 1988, will remain in Libya, an official with the rebel government said.

2011-08-29.title: In Pakistan, Court Moves to Punish Ex-President
2011-08-29.abstract: A judge ordered that former President Pervez Musharraf’s property be seized for his failure to respond to subpoenas related to the assassination of former Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto.

2011-08-29.title: 28 Are Killed in Bombing at a Mosque in Baghdad
2011-08-29.abstract: A recent spike in suicide bombings has heightened fears among Iraqis that security is deteriorating as the United States prepares to withdraw its troops.

2011-08-29.title: Enigmatic in Power, Qaddafi Is Elusive at Large
2011-08-29.abstract: As his capital fell last week, Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi and his family evaporated from Tripoli, once their bastion of power that is now a city being reconstructed.

2011-08-29.title: U.S. Tactics in Libya May Be a Model for Other Efforts
2011-08-29.abstract: The actions in Libya helped shape an Obama doctrine that supports using force when justified but not going it alone.

2011-08-29.title: Battle for Libya | August 28, 2011
2011-08-29.abstract: Libyans continued to search the Bab al-Aziziya complex and rebel fighters continued their slow push toward Surt.

2011-08-30.title: For Egypt, a Symbol of Boldness Is Disputed
2011-08-30.abstract: A flag switch at the Israeli Embassy during a protest has been claimed by two men.

2011-08-30.title: WikiLeaks Leaves Names of Diplomatic Sources in Cables
2011-08-30.abstract: The antisecrecy organization WikiLeaks published nearly 134,000 diplomatic cables, including many that name confidential sources.

2011-08-30.title: Increasing Drug Violence in Brazil
2011-08-30.abstract: The number of murders in Bahia state grew 430 percent to 4,709 between 1999 and 2008.

2011-08-30.title: As Prosperity Rises in Brazil’s Northeast, So Does Drug Violence
2011-08-30.abstract: Brazil’s economic boom is causing drug-related violence to migrate to the northeast as traffickers seek new markets, experts say.

2011-08-30.title: China Takes Aim at Rural Influx
2011-08-30.abstract: Many schools attended by migrants have been destroyed recently, but some parents and administrators view the bulldozing as an exercise in population control.

2011-08-31.title: Admiral Defends Use of Elite Unit in Calamitous Raid
2011-08-31.abstract: Adm. William H. McRaven said “there was nothing unusual” about the Aug. 6 mission in Afghanistan during which an American helicopter was shot down.

2011-08-31.title: Panel Hears Grim Details of Venereal Disease Tests
2011-08-31.abstract: An American-run study from the 1940s, brought to light last year, involved gruesome treatment of Guatemalans.

2011-08-31.title: Tripoli Divided as Rebels Jostle to Fill Power Vacuum
2011-08-31.abstract: A week after rebels stormed Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi’s former stronghold, much remained divided into fiefs, each controlled by quasi-independent brigades.

2011-08-31.title: Surge in Global Migration Expands Scale of an Aid Group’s Influence
2011-08-31.abstract: The rapid growth of the International Organization for Migration is a sign that migration has outgrown most countries’ ability to manage on their own.

2011-08-31.title: With Help Online, French Farmers Now Playing the Field
2011-08-31.abstract: A lack of love in the French countryside has helped propel the growth of online dating sites reserved for farmers.
